what: Apples-to-apples re-run of the 12-arm batched sweep AFTER the two throughput levers landed (PR #74: batched drafter forward + BatchGraphRunner batched-decode CUDA graphs). Three passes on one 6x5090 EU ring (same shape as batched-sweep-eagle-20260710: light head [0:2] + 5x12L): v1-off = levers-1-only (fp8 wire, batched graphs off), v1-on = full new build (fp8 wire, graphs on), v2-on = the OLD receipt's numerics env (NO fp8 wire, graphs on, kv 4096). Every arm receipt-attested; per-arm graph route ack-verified (the reset_batch graph-arm stamping from the adversarial review).
ring_v1: 6 stages CZ->BG HU IT NO SE-shaped: DK(head,[0:2]) BG[2:14] HU[14:26] IT[26:38] NO[38:50] SE[50:62], M25_EAGLE=1 M25_BATCH=8 kv 8192, M25_CUDA_GRAPH=1 M25_STATIC_KV=1 M25_FP8_WIRE=1, K=8, max_new 96, receipts ON, SWEEP_GRAPH_ARMS=off,on with untimed per-B graph warmups
ring_v2: same ring, head replaced (DK host CUDA/uvm died mid-session -> destroyed; CZ[0:2] replacement), NO fp8 wire (the old receipt's env), kv-maxlen 4096 (frees the tail brim: B=8 graphs then capture cleanly; runtime attention identical - reads are bucket-clamped either way), graphs on

headline_bcurve.note: B=1/2/4/8 mixed-content agg tok/s. v2-on is the honest old-receipt comparison; v1-on adds fp8 wire (2x payload cut) on top of the levers.

findings: 1. CONTENT-g REPRODUCES THE OLD RECEIPT EXACTLY at the old numerics env (v2: summarize 5.80==5.80, tools 3.73==3.73, qa 2.38==2.38, prose 2.16==2.16, mix-B1 2.46==2.46, mix-B8 2.97~=2.95): the model's greedy outputs byte-match the old run, live-validating that the batched drafter forward changed NOTHING about drafting quality or committed content - the pure-wall-clock claim, proven on the ring.

2. THE DRAFTING TAX IS DEAD. Old mix-B1 round 1158ms -> v2-on 458ms (same env, levers only). v1 lever split at B=4: off 1398ms/round -> on 787ms/round (graphs), and the serial ~0.25s/stream drafting term is gone at every B (B=1 round 212ms on the fp8 build INCLUDING drafting).

3. WHAT BINDS NOW: WAN PAYLOAD, linear in B. At the old env (bf16 wire) mix-B4 rounds are 1607ms ~= the old 1630ms - the round is transport-payload-bound (EAGLE aux = 3 layers x [B,K+1,H] dominates; levers can't move it). fp8 wire halves it: mix-B4 787ms -> agg 14.54. The 20-agg bar moved from DRAFTING-bound to TRANSPORT-bound.

4. fp8 WIRE IS A ~2x THROUGHPUT LEVER *AND* A NUMERICS ENV: it shifts greedy content and the measured content-g band (mix 3.55 bf16 -> 2.48 fp8; summarize 5.80 -> 2.03). g_batched must be quoted per wire mode. The operative admission number 2.5 now rests on the fp8 measurement directly (2.48) instead of a drafter-tax discount.

5. Brim behavior worked as designed: v1 (kv 8192) tail free-VRAM guard refused B=8 capture (1.3GB free < 1.5GB estimate) -> 5/6-graphed arm, honestly counted in graph_arm; v2 (kv 4096) zero capture failures, B=8 fully graphed.

6. qa-B4 in v2 stalled (4.9s/round, receipts still valid) - a WAN-leg outlier on big bf16 frames, footnoted NOT averaged; the same content ran 18.05 agg on the fp8 build. mix-B4-hybrid v1-on row lost with the dead DK box (duplicate-content arm of mix-B4).
7. All arms across all three passes: receipts_ok true (6 stages signed, nonce-fresh, fail-closed).
bar_verdict: mix-B4 >= 25 / mix-B8 >= 50 NOT met. Best measured: B4 14.54, B8 15.48 (fp8 build). Honest decomposition: at B=4 fp8 the 787ms round is ~200ms ring floor + ~450ms payload+coordinator + <150ms drafting; the remaining lever class is transport (aux slimming, depth>1 overlap), not compute.
next_levers_quantified: 1. AUX SLIMMING (lossless): the head forwards the drafted rows ('tids') so the TAIL can slice the returned aux to each stream's ACCEPTED prefix +1 (the coordinator only extend()s committed positions). HONEST leg math: this trims the RETURN leg's 3 aux layers (~60% cut at typical g) ~= 10-15% of round bytes, NOT the 2-3x first estimated. The BIGGER prize in the same analysis: L1's aux is produced by the head stage ON THE COORDINATOR'S OWN BOX yet rides 6 WAN legs (aux-layer-legs at this shape: L1=6, L30=3, L58=1 vs activations=6) - delivering it head-locally would cut ~60% of aux bytes-on-wire (~35-40% of round bytes at B=4 bf16). Design-panelled; the protocol seam is the follow-up.
2. fp8 wire as the standard batched env (2x payload) - already built; quote g_batched per wire mode.
3. Depth>1 stale-context EAGLE: drafting is now cheap enough that pipelining rounds again hides WAN (was pointless when drafting serialized).
4. B=8 full graphs need kv<=4096 on 32GB tails (or 48GB tails).
spec_impact: Operative g_batched stays 2.5 but re-derived: measured fp8-wire content-mix g = 2.48 (band 1.9-3.5); bf16-wire g = 3.55 (band 2.2-5.8, == old receipt). The drafter-serialization discount no longer applies (tax removed).
rows_v1: see rows_v1 array; passes tagged by graph_arm.graph (false=lever-1-only, true=full build)
rows_v1_data_file: recovered from the session monitor (23/24 rows); raw JSON rows follow

v1.note: tail eager (B8 capture refused by VRAM guard at kv 8192): 5/6-graphed

v2_on_old_env.note: WAN-leg stall outlier (4.9s/round), receipts valid; footnoted not averaged
133ms/round graphed floor vs old 220ms eager floor

ops_notes: Session ops: 3 HF pull stalls (xet hang) fixed by kill+resume kick cycles; DK head host CUDA/uvm died on stage relaunch (reboot + stop/start did not recover -> destroyed, CZ replacement head bootstrapped in ~6min); one zombie instance from rent_pool caught at teardown. instances-v1==0 verified at session end.
